purpose: Convert the post-Stage-7 process loop into a repository-owned product roadmap control layer: record product gaps, group the next product work into larger related batches, and guard the roadmap against drift from project-memory and the self-hosted product boundary.

stages.title: Product gap register
stages.outcome: A machine-readable register records confirmed product areas, remaining gaps, and evidence paths without claiming external go-live approval.

stages.title: Next product batch planner
stages.outcome: A dry-run roadmap report groups the next product work into larger related batches with at least three stages each.

stages.title: Product roadmap drift guard
stages.outcome: A guard fails when the roadmap manifest, reporter, docs, workflow, package scripts, preflight-all entry, and project-memory no longer describe the same next product plan.

batchPlanPolicy.samePullRequestJustification: All three stages define the transition from process automation back to product delivery: identify remaining product gaps, plan the next larger product batches, and guard the roadmap against drift.

confirmedProductAreas.summary: Self-hosted backend, PostgreSQL, local object storage, health/readiness, OpenAPI, auth/RBAC, clinical data, ops, and product readiness contracts exist in repository-owned code.
Production mode routes are wired to self-hosted API contracts while demo/dev keeps mock data separated.
Booking requests, external intake imports, available slots, and slot confirmation contracts are represented as self-hosted product boundaries.
Release, archive, retention, and project-memory control artifacts are stored in git while live evidence and operator decisions remain outside git.

remainingGaps.summary: Adapter delivery/ops packages exist, but concrete clinic CRM connector implementations and scheduler mapping remain future product work.
Available-slot and booking-confirmation contracts exist; continuous production sync, conflict handling, and operator reconciliation need the next product batch.
Clinical assessment, conclusion, and report contracts exist; final production reporting UX, review workflow, and export hardening remain planned.
Device registry and worker contracts exist; production device onboarding, diagnostics, and operator handoff need a focused product hardening batch.
Server bootstrap and migration hardening exist; a consolidated operator handbook for install, backup, restore, and incident exercises remains planned.

nextProductBatches.title: CRM inbound adapter implementation
nextProductBatches.focus: Implement operator-owned CRM/ad-source inbound adapter contracts, normalization, and safe import audit flow.
nextProductBatches.boundary: Adapter code must run in the self-hosted deployment or an operator-owned connector process; no managed runtime dependency is allowed.

nextProductBatches.title: Appointment availability sync and booking confirmation
nextProductBatches.focus: Turn cached clinic slots and booking requests into production availability sync, conflict handling, and booking confirmation workflows.
nextProductBatches.boundary: Source systems may provide data, but Dermatolog Pro keeps its operational database and decision state self-hosted.

nextProductBatches.title: Clinical reporting completion
nextProductBatches.focus: Complete production clinical reporting UX, report review, and safe report export from self-hosted backend contracts.
nextProductBatches.boundary: No raw patient names, storage paths, signed URLs, or tokens may appear in generated sync reports.

nextProductBatches.title: Device Bridge production hardening
nextProductBatches.focus: Harden device onboarding, worker diagnostics, command safety drills, and operator-facing device support runbooks.
nextProductBatches.boundary: Browser hardware APIs remain out of the web UI unless a future explicit operator-approved bridge design is added.

nextProductBatches.title: Server operations handbook
nextProductBatches.focus: Create a single operator handbook for install, backup, restore, upgrade, rollback, and incident drills.
nextProductBatches.boundary: Operational evidence stays outside git; repository artifacts only describe redacted manifests and checklists.

lovableSyncVerification.expectedConfirmation: Confirmed: Stage 7J-7L synced from main, no conflicts.
